title: Cloudflare Durable Objects — spec → production DO
description: Describe a Durable Object in plain language; the agent implements it in production-ready TypeScript on Cloudflare Workers, writes the matching wrangler.jsonc, and self-reviews the code against Cloudflare's documented gotchas (hibernation clears in-memory state, setTimeout doesn't survive eviction, only one alarm per DO, await is a yield point). Converted, with attribution, from Cloudflare's own Durable Objects reference.

cta.label: Fork and run
cta.note: Runs on Jetty's managed sandbox. No setup. Free for your first 10 runs.

example_outputs.title: ChatRoom — WebSockets + persistent counter + idle cleanup
example_outputs.summary: A multi-room chat DO: WebSocket Hibernation API for broadcast, a SQLite-backed message counter incremented with an atomic INSERT ... ON CONFLICT upsert, and a setAlarm()-debounced 30-minute idle purge that rebuilds its schema on reconnect. Self-review guards 9 gotchas.

input.spec: ChatRoom DO: WebSocket broadcast per room, persistent message count at GET /count, delete room state after 30 min idle.

outputs.description: The ChatRoom Durable Object — hibernation API, atomic SQL counter, setAlarm idle cleanup.
Self-review against Cloudflare's documented gotchas (9 Risk/Fix entries).

example_outputs.title: Scheduler — two recurring jobs from one alarm
example_outputs.summary: The non-obvious case: a DO has exactly one alarm, but this Scheduler runs two independent timers (5-min heartbeat + 30-min session expiry). It multiplexes them — store each job's next-due time, arm the single alarm to Math.min(...), and recompute drift-free in alarm(). All schedule state persists, so it survives eviction.

input.spec: Scheduler DO: heartbeat every 5 min + session-expiry every 30 min, both surviving eviction; one alarm, multiplexed.

outputs.description: The Scheduler Durable Object — single-alarm multiplexing via next-due keys + Math.min.
Worker config with the SQLite-class migration.
Self-review leading with the 'Only One Alarm Allowed' gotcha.

inputs.label: Spec
inputs.description: The Durable Object to build, in plain language — its behavior, what state it persists, any scheduling, and the HTTP/WebSocket surface.

attribution.author: Cloudflare (github.com/cloudflare/skills)
attribution.note: The Rules of Durable Objects, the API notes, the patterns, the wrangler configuration, and the gotchas are Cloudflare's. This runbook turns that reference into an executable 'spec → production DO' build that self-reviews the generated code against those gotchas.

caveats: The two shipped examples are the WITH-gotchas arm of an A/B experiment (does injecting Cloudflare's documented gotchas change the generated code?). Both correctly avoid every gotcha — see the experiment report for the full result and the cautionary methodology note.

Targets TypeScript on Cloudflare Workers only. The DO + wrangler.jsonc are produced and self-reviewed but not deployed (no `wrangler deploy` in the sandbox) — evaluation is static/structural, not a live Workers run.

Counter strategy varies by run: some use a SQLite atomic upsert (shown here), others a KV value restored under blockConcurrencyWhile — both are race-safe. Treat the example as one correct shape, not the only one.
compatibility_date and the migration type (new_sqlite_classes vs new_classes) matter — the runbook sets them, but verify against your account's current Workers compatibility before deploying.

experiment.question: Do Cloudflare's documented 'gotchas' make Claude Sonnet 4.6 write better Durable Object code?
experiment.design: Same spec, two arms — WITH the gotchas injected vs WITHOUT — 3 runs each across 2 tasks (12 runs total). The gotchas section was the only variable.
experiment.result: Correctness was a tie: all 12 runs avoided every gotcha, including the non-obvious single-alarm pattern. The gotchas' value showed up as traceability, canonical idioms, and surfacing subtle config gotchas — not as fixed mistakes.

handoff.name: cf-do-with
handoff.eval_runs: 12/12 production runs green across both arms (cf-do-with + cf-do-without, 3×2 tasks); every run's validation_report.json overall_passed: true
